name: Basic Lomein
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak dried mushrooms.
2. Slice roast pork, celery, cabbage, and bamboo shoots.
3. Blanch bean sprouts.
4. Stem snow peas.
5. Shred ginger root and crush garlic.
6. Heat oil in a pan.
7. Add ginger root and garlic; stir-fry until lightly browned.
8. Add pork and stir-fry for a few more times.
9. Add sherry to blend the flavors.
10. Add all vegetables except snow peas and stir-fry for 1 minute longer.
11. Add stock and heat quickly.
12. Cook, covered, for 3 minutes over medium heat.
13. Stir in snow peas.
14. Combine the mixture with tossed noodles.
Variations:
- Substitute cooked beef, ham, chicken, duck, crabmeat, lobster, or shrimp.
- Add shredded green onion to the garlic and ginger mixture.
- Use Shaoxing wine instead of sherry for a more authentic flavor.
To cook noodles: Prepare boiling water with a little salt. Add noodles and cook until barely done. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking. Separate noodles with a fork, then toss them with a little sesame oil or peanut oil and set aside until ready to use in the stir-fry. Approximately one pound of noodles is needed for this recipe.
